sourceMovie.title: The BFG
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The BFG (2016) stuck because it commits to its adventure premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Steven Spielberg's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The adventure and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Alice in Wonderland
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The BFG clicked for you, Alice in Wonderland (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and family, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The BFG?
faq.answer: Try Alice in Wonderland, Maleficent, Miss Peregrine's Home for Peculiar Children—each shares DNA with The BFG while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The BFG?
faq.answer: The BFG (2016) is best described as Adventure, Family, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The BFG worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
